channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Nicaragua confirms death in custody of Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera
item.description: Rivera spent nearly three years in detention, cut off from the outside world, leading advocates to fear for his welfare.

item.title: Can AI cure loneliness? South Korea’s robot companions for seniors
item.description: South Korea is using AI-powered companion dolls to help tackle loneliness in its ageing population

item.title: New Jersey orders curfew after immigration protests escalate
item.description: New Jersey orders curfew after immigration protests escalate.

item.title: Ethiopia to vote in first elections since Tigray peace deal
item.description: Four years after the formal end of Ethiopia's Tigray war, Ethiopians will be voting in general elections.

item.title: Kohli powers Bengaluru to ‘stuff of dreams’ back-to-back IPL titles
item.description: Having never previously won the Indian Premier League, Virat Kohli seals consecutive titles as Gujarat Titans defeated.

item.title: As Ethiopia votes, its deepening human rights crisis must be addressed
item.description: Continuing internal conflicts and deepening repression are casting a long shadow over the general election.

item.title: Ethiopia’s election is about affirming national commitment to democracy
item.description: A national vote alone can't address all challenges in the country, but it can be a step toward democratic consolidation.

item.title: Newark mayor imposes curfew at Delaney Hall immigration detention centre
item.description: New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill has called for the establishment of protest zones to minimise clashes with police.

item.title: What’s at stake in Ethiopia’s elections?
item.description: Ethiopia's governing party is seeking to cement its grip on power amid a fragmented electorate.

item.title: Explosives store blast kills dozens in Myanmar village
item.description: Explosives store blast kills dozens in Myanmar village.

item.title: Building collapses in New Delhi, some people rescued, more feared trapped
item.description: Nine people have reportedly been rescued from the rubble of a collapsed five-storey building in New Delhi, India.

item.title: US measure to deepen Israel military cooperation faces bipartisan pushback
item.description: Democrat Ro Khanna and Republican Thomas Massie say they will try to remove the pro-Israel section from US defence bill.

item.title: US Congress moves to deepen military ties with Israel: Why it matters
item.description: Analysts say the plan would weave Israeli military interests more deeply into the fabric of US national security policy.

item.title: The largest Israeli advance into Lebanon in years
item.description: Israel’s military presence in southern Lebanon has expanded dramatically.

item.title: Polls open in Colombia’s pivotal presidential election
item.description: Colombians headed to the polls to choose a new president in a high-stakes election.

item.title: Dozens killed in explosives depot blast in northeast Myanmar
item.description: At least 46 people have been killed and 70 wounded in explosion in rebel-held area, local media report.

item.title: The Bazaar of Return in Aida Refugee Camp
item.description: The Bazaar of Return in Aida Refugee Camp

item.title: Ethiopia’s election: Parties, coalitions and candidates explained
item.description: Over 50 million Ethiopians are registered to vote, with youth and women forming a significant share of the electorate.

item.title: Iran ⁠restores some gas production at South Pars facility
item.description: Production restored at three offshore platforms after Israeli attacks, Pars Oil and Gas Company CEO tells Iranian media.

item.title: Colombians head to the polls to choose President Gustavo Petro’s successor
item.description: The contest is largely a three-horse race between a left-wing senator, a businessman and a right-wing lawmaker.

item.title: Juan Manuel Santos on Colombia’s peace process, 10 years on
item.description: The former Colombian president reflects on peace, conflict and the return of violence.

item.title: Germany World Cup 2026 team preview: Players to watch, group and squad list
item.description: The 2014 champs exited in the group stages of the last two World Cups but look a stronger force under Julian Nagelsmann.

item.title: Rescuers race to save two people still trapped in cave in Laos
item.description: Rescuers face heavy rains, equipment failures in search for two people trapped in central Laos cave by flash floods.

item.title: Could Democrats be shut out of the California governor’s race?
item.description: California is a Democrat stronghold, but thanks to the state's jungle primary, Republicans are frontrunners to advance.

item.title: Iran reinstates some internet access but restrictions remain for most
item.description: Not all data centres are back online while internet protocols remain blocked, restricted or 'whitelisted'.
